title: Real Estate Investing Strategies That Generate $1,000+ Monthly Cashflow
guestBio: Dave Lundgren is a real estate investor, transformational coach, and bestselling author of Loveness with over 25 years of experience. A former U.S. Army chaplain, he has built a national lease option platform that averages 59% total returns for over 2,100 investors.
excerpt: Discover why traditional rental properties are failing and how lease options and syndications can deliver 14% cash-on-cash returns. Dave Lundgren reveals the strategies most investors never hear about, plus a hidden IRS loophole that could wipe out your tax bill.
metaDescription: Learn real estate investing strategies that generate $1,000+ monthly cashflow. Discover lease options, syndications & tax loopholes most investors miss.
keyTakeaways: Lease options outperform traditional rentals with higher cashflow, better tenants, and zero maintenance responsibilities.
Real estate has created more millionaires than any other asset class, but the team matters more than the market when investing.
Women control over $10 trillion in U.S. household financial assets but are often missing out on wealth-building real estate deals.
Creative financing strategies allow you to purchase real estate without traditional bank loans, unlocking deals others cannot access.
Cashflow protects you during market downturns while appreciation should be viewed as a bonus, not the primary investment strategy.
The hidden IRS Section 168-179 loophole can potentially eliminate your tax bill through accelerated depreciation on real estate investments.
Real estate syndications allow passive investors to participate in larger deals with professional management and higher returns than REITs.

pullQuotes.text: Real estate has created more millionaires than any other asset class
The team matters more than the market
Cashflow is king and appreciation alone is risky

quiz.title: What's Your Real Estate Investor Personality?
quiz.description: Discover your natural approach to building wealth through real estate and which strategies align with your strengths and lifestyle.

A.name: Strategic Partner
A.description: You excel at building relationships and finding win-win solutions. Your collaborative nature and ability to see multiple perspectives make you perfect for creative financing deals and syndications. You understand that the best investments come through trust and partnership, not just numbers. This episode will show you how to leverage your relationship skills to access deals that others miss and why your collaborative approach is actually a competitive advantage in real estate investing.

B.name: Calculated Analyst
B.description: You love diving deep into data and understanding every aspect of a deal before making decisions. Your thorough approach and attention to detail make you excellent at evaluating syndications and understanding complex investment structures. You prefer passive investments where you can analyze the team and metrics without day-to-day involvement. This episode will give you the specific metrics and evaluation criteria you need to confidently assess real estate opportunities and understand the tax advantages that amplify returns.

C.name: Hands-On Builder
C.description: You enjoy being actively involved in your investments and aren't afraid of putting in work to maximize returns. Your willingness to learn and take action makes you ideal for active strategies like lease options or direct property investment. You see real estate as both an investment and a way to create value through your own efforts. This episode will show you how to move beyond traditional rentals to strategies that reward your active involvement while eliminating common landlord headaches.

D.name: Freedom Seeker
D.description: You want your money to work for you without consuming your time or energy. Your focus on lifestyle and financial freedom makes you perfect for passive real estate investments and syndications. You understand that true wealth means having options and not being tied down by management responsibilities. This episode will reveal how to achieve higher returns than traditional investments while maintaining the freedom and flexibility that matter most to you.

options.text: Creating win-win opportunities and building long-term relationships
Achieving consistent returns while minimizing risk through careful analysis

questions.question: When learning about new investment strategies, you prefer to:

options.text: Connect with successful investors who can mentor and guide you
Study the track records, metrics, and detailed performance data
Understand the hands-on process so you can evaluate and manage deals yourself
Find turnkey solutions where experts handle the complex details

content:
You've been told that real estate investing means becoming a landlord, dealing with midnight maintenance calls, and hoping your property appreciates over time. But what if there was a completely different approach that could generate $1,000+ in monthly cashflow from the same property that traditional rentals struggle to make $200 on?

In this episode of The Manage Her, host Aimee Rickabus sits down with real estate investor and former Army chaplain Dave Lundgren to expose the strategies that most investors never hear about. With over 25 years of experience and a track record of averaging 59% total returns for more than 2,100 investors, Dave reveals why the conventional wisdom about real estate investing is keeping you from building the wealth you deserve.

The Problem with Traditional Rental Properties

If you've ever considered buying rental property, you've probably run the numbers and felt disappointed. Traditional rentals in today's market are struggling to generate meaningful cashflow after accounting for mortgage payments, taxes, insurance, and maintenance costs. Dave explains that this is exactly why savvy investors are turning to alternative strategies like lease options.

Unlike traditional rentals where you own the property and rent it out, lease options involve giving someone the right to purchase a property at a predetermined price within a specific timeframe. The tenant pays option fees upfront, higher monthly payments, and takes care of all maintenance. This approach delivers multiple income streams while eliminating the headaches of traditional landlording.

Why Women Are Missing Out on Real Estate Wealth

Here's a statistic that will shock you: women control over $10 trillion in U.S. household financial assets, yet they're often excluded from the most lucrative real estate investment opportunities. Dave points out that many of the best deals happen through relationships and networks that have historically been male-dominated.

But here's what gives me hope: women are uniquely positioned to excel in real estate investing because of their natural relationship-building abilities and collaborative approach. The key is understanding that you don't have to go it alone or figure everything out from scratch. Real estate syndications allow you to pool your resources with other investors and benefit from professional management and institutional-quality deals.

The Power of Syndications vs Going Solo

Real estate syndications are essentially group investments where multiple investors pool their money to purchase larger, more profitable properties. Think of it as the difference between buying a single rental house versus owning a piece of a 200-unit apartment complex with professional management.

The advantages are compelling: higher returns, professional management, economies of scale, and the ability to invest passively without becoming a landlord. Dave's syndications have consistently delivered returns that far exceed what individual investors typically achieve on their own.

The Hidden Tax Advantage Most Investors Miss

One of the most powerful aspects of real estate investing is the tax benefits, and Dave reveals a little-known IRS provision called Section 168-179 that could potentially eliminate your entire tax bill. Through accelerated depreciation, real estate investors can write off significant portions of their investment in the first year, creating substantial tax savings that amplify returns.

This isn't about sketchy tax avoidance schemes. These are legitimate strategies that the IRS specifically created to encourage real estate investment. The challenge is that most people simply don't know these opportunities exist because their accountants aren't specialized in real estate investing.

Creative Financing: Buying Without Banks

Perhaps the most eye-opening part of this conversation is Dave's explanation of creative financing strategies. You don't always need to go through traditional banks to purchase real estate. Seller financing, lease options, and other creative structures can allow you to control valuable real estate with minimal upfront capital.

This is where relationship skills become incredibly valuable. Creative financing is about finding win-win solutions for property owners who need flexibility, not just cash. Maybe they need steady income, tax advantages, or a delayed sale for personal reasons. When you understand their motivations, you can structure deals that benefit everyone involved.

Aimee's Personal Real Estate Journey

During the episode, Aimee shares her own experience with real estate investing, including a property purchase in Laguna Beach that taught her valuable lessons about market timing and intuition. Her story illustrates that even successful entrepreneurs can struggle with real estate decisions when they don't have the right knowledge and support systems.

This vulnerability is exactly why having experienced partners and mentors is so crucial. Real estate investing isn't just about numbers and spreadsheets. It's about understanding markets, timing, and having the emotional intelligence to make decisions under uncertainty.

Your Next Steps in Real Estate Investing

The most important takeaway from this conversation is that real estate investing doesn't have to be overwhelming or require you to become an expert overnight. Whether you're interested in active strategies like lease options or passive investments through syndications, the key is starting with education and finding the right team.

Dave emphasizes that the team matters more than the market. You want partners with proven track records, transparent communication, and alignment with your goals and values. Don't just focus on promised returns. Look at their experience, their investor relations, and their approach to risk management.

Real estate has created more millionaires than any other asset class, and these strategies prove that you don't need massive amounts of capital to get started. You need knowledge, the right relationships, and the courage to think beyond traditional rental properties. The question isn't whether you can afford to invest in real estate. The question is whether you can afford not to.
